# Risk Register — 0000003-hook-based-enforcement

*Generated by spec-agent. Every risk is specific to this feature.*

---

## Risks

| ID | Category | Description | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation | Status |
|----|----------|------------|------------|--------|-----------|--------|
| R-001 | technical | **Coordinated deploy ordering for MCP `"ship"` enum.** If the ship-agent SKILL.md is merged before the structured-telemetry-mcp schema update is deployed, telemetry validation for `phase: "ship"` will be rejected. **Functional impact is telemetry gap only** — hook scripts exit 0 on schema rejection and never block execution. | medium | low | (1) CI check on the ship-agent PR POSTs a test event with `"phase": "ship"` to the MCP endpoint; build fails if rejected — enforces deploy order automatically. (2) Document deploy order in execution plan. (3) Telemetry hook silent-failure behaviour (REQ-004) contains the blast radius. | open |
| R-002 | technical | **OpenCode plugin API stability.** The `@planifest/opencode-hooks` plugin relies on OpenCode's plugin interface, which is in active development. API breaking changes could break Tier 2 enforcement without warning. | medium | medium | Pin OpenCode version in setup script. Document minimum supported version. Flag as a maintenance risk in the plugin README. | open |
| R-003 | technical | **Codex CLI `features.codex_hooks` flag may not exist in older versions.** If a user has an older Codex CLI install, the setup script may silently write a flag that has no effect. | medium | low | Setup script checks Codex CLI version before writing flag. Prints a minimum version warning if below the threshold (to be determined during codegen). | open |
| R-004 | technical | **Hook script 3-second timeout insufficient for slow network environments.** In high-latency environments (e.g. VPN, remote dev), the telemetry POST may consistently time out and never emit. | low | low | Timeout is a non-blocking silent exit; the session is never affected. Accepted as a telemetry completeness trade-off. Users on slow networks may see gaps in telemetry data. | accepted |
| R-005 | technical | **Flag-file guard relies on `PLANIFEST_SESSION_ID` env var.** If the session ID is not set (e.g. tool does not provide it), the guard would deduplicate against an empty session ID — only one `phase_start` fires per phase across all sessions. | medium | low | `emit-phase-start.mjs` reads/creates `{cwd}/.claude/.planifest-session` as a project-scoped session ID fallback. File is created with a generated UUID on first hook invocation; read on all subsequent invocations in the same project. Consistent across process restarts. Deleted by ship-agent at Phase 7. | mitigated |
| R-006 | operational | **gate-write.mjs design.md parsing fragility.** If the component paths section in `design.md` uses a non-standard heading format, the path extraction regex fails and the gate falls back to pass-through (not block). This is the safe failure mode but means enforcement is silently disabled. | low | medium | Define a canonical heading format for component paths in design.md. Codegen-agent must follow this format. Add a format validation step to gate-write.mjs that logs a warning (but not a block) when the section is unrecognised. | open |
| R-007 | operational | **Resume detection misidentifies phase if artefacts from a prior aborted feature exist.** If `plan/current/` was not archived after a failed ship attempt, leftover artefacts could cause the orchestrator to resume at the wrong phase for a new feature. | low | high | (1) **`.feature-id` marker:** ship-agent writes `plan/current/.feature-id` at the start of Phase 7. Resume detection reads this file; if it contains a different feature ID than the current session, the orchestrator warns the human before proceeding. (2) **Two-phase archive:** ship-agent copies to archive then deletes (not moves). A failed copy leaves artefacts in both places — safe and idempotent. Re-run adds `-{n}` suffix and proceeds. | mitigated |
| R-008 | security | **Hook scripts execute arbitrary Node.js with access to the filesystem.** A malicious or corrupted hook registration (e.g. injected into `.claude/settings.json`) could execute attacker-controlled code. | low | high | Hook scripts are shipped in the framework repo (not user-supplied). Setup scripts write hook paths that point to the framework directory only. Reviewed in the security phase. | open |
| R-009 | security | **`PLANIFEST_TELEMETRY_URL` set to an attacker-controlled endpoint could exfiltrate session metadata.** The telemetry hooks POST session_id, phase, and timestamps to the configured URL. | low | medium | The URL is set by the project owner in their environment. No sensitive data (credentials, file content) is included in the telemetry payload. Document that `PLANIFEST_TELEMETRY_URL` should only be set to a trusted endpoint. | open |
| R-010 | compliance | **Assumption: hook script output format for `additionalContext` matches Claude Code's UserPromptSubmit hook response schema.** If Claude Code updates its hook response schema, check-design.mjs may inject context in a format the tool no longer reads. | medium | medium | Pin Claude Code version in documentation. The check-design.mjs response schema is based on the hooks reference reviewed during design (DD). Re-verify during codegen phase. | open |

---

## Assumptions

Assumptions made during requirements phase. Each has likelihood: medium.

| ID | Assumption | Impact if Wrong |
|----|-----------|----------------|
| A-001 | Node.js ≥18 is available in the shell environment where hooks execute (required for `globalThis.fetch` or `http` module with AbortController). | Hook scripts fail at runtime on older Node.js. Mitigation: add a Node.js version check at the start of each script, print a warning, and exit 0. |
| A-002 | The `UserPromptSubmit` hook `additionalContext` return value is displayed to the agent before the LLM processes the prompt (not after). | Scope injection would be ineffective — the agent sees the prompt before the context appears. Verify with Claude Code hooks reference during codegen. |
| A-003 | OpenCode's Bun runtime is accessible as `bun` in the PATH when the opencode plugin is loaded. | The `@planifest/opencode-hooks` plugin cannot spawn `bun build` or `bun run`. Setup script must verify Bun is available. |
| A-004 | Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline hook systems use a `cwd` field in the hook input envelope that represents the project root. | Adapter scripts cannot resolve `plan/current/design.md` relative to the project root. Fallback: use `process.cwd()` if `cwd` is absent. |
